g.text: : Table variable with one CustID per row
- Used for joining with other tables
: Get baseline classification and probation info
COUNT(DISTINCT CAST(t.BetDate AS DATE)) AS ActiveDaysDuringProbation,
WHERE t.TransactionDate BETWEEN c.ProbationStartDate AND GETDATE()
- Date range: First and last bet
THEN 'Improved' -- Less loss or more win
THEN 'Deteriorated' -- More loss or less win
WHEN CurrentCategoryID = PA AND PerformanceTrend = 'Improved' THEN
WHEN CurrentCategoryID = PA AND PerformanceTrend = 'Maintained' THEN
NewCategoryID = PA -- No improvement, extend probation
WHEN CurrentCategoryID = PA AND PerformanceTrend = 'Deteriorated' THEN
NewCategoryID = PA -- Worse, maintain PA, extend probation
WHEN CurrentCategoryID = PotentialPA AND PerformanceTrend = 'Improved' THEN
ELSE NewCategoryID = PotentialPA -- Still some concern
WHEN CurrentCategoryID = PotentialPA AND PerformanceTrend = 'Deteriorated' THEN
JOIN CategoryMaster cat ON cat.CategoryID = pa.NewCategoryID
- Focus on customer behavior during probation
